title: ranunculus acris subsp. japonicus
intro: Ranunculus acris subsp. japonicus, also known as the Japanese buttercup, is a plant species belonging to the buttercup family. It is known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, which are derived from its roots and rhizomes. The plant has various health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and has been used to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of tinctures, decoctions, and infusions, and is also used as a topical cream to alleviate skin irritations.
